article.body_preview:
The Dhaka Chamber of Commerce and Industry (DCCI) today urged the government to focus on ensuring uninterrupted and affordable energy for both local and export-oriented manufacturing units to further accelerate Bangladesh's economic recovery in 2023.

Also, emphasis should be placed on improving the ease of doing business and developing an infrastructural environment that is conducive for attracting local and foreign investment, according to a press release from the trade body.

In addition, the

llm_connections.evidence_quote: devaluation of the taka against the US Dollar has challenged the country's financial sector, resulting in some negative impacts on the import of energy, industrial raw materials
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly links devaluation of the taka to negative impacts on the import of energy and industrial raw materials, directly addressing exchange-rate effects on imports as defined by the feature.
